review_analysis.assessment: The high REVIEW count is mainly driven by conservative classification/exposure checks and normal Morningstar exposure refreshes, not by forbidden fields or actual write blockers.

likely_reasons_for_high_review_count: Comparator is intentionally strict with classification_v2 and portfolio_exposure_v2 material changes.
Most REVIEW rows include exposure refreshes across allocation, sectors, regions, style, credit and duration buckets.
480 rows include classification_or_asset_type_changed, often semantic/classification tension rather than a confirmed bad proposal.
Only 18 rows have incomplete proposed relevant fields, so missing data is not the main driver.
diagnosis.small_batch_for_future_write_gate: 83 LOW_RISK_UPDATE rows only, after human sample review. Exclude REVIEW_REQUIRED and NOT_FOUND_IN_FUNDS_V3 from any automatic write gate.
comparator_adjustment_notes: Separate pure Morningstar refresh fields from semantic classification changes.
Downgrade normal portfolio_exposure_v2 drift to LOW_RISK only when asset_type and category remain stable.
Keep classification_v2.asset_type, asset_subtype and material cross-asset moves in REVIEW.
Handle new/missing historical fields separately from actual conflicting values.

recommended_next_step: Prepare a small read-only write-gate candidate list from the 83 LOW_RISK_UPDATE rows; adjust/split comparator logic before considering the 534 REVIEW_REQUIRED rows.
